explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to text segments describing **urgent, disruptive, or crisis events** (such as natural disasters, accidents, political conflicts, emergency actions, or dramatic incidents).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across multiple languages (Dutch, Italian, Portuguese, German, Romanian, Arabic, Greek, Spanish, Russian, etc.) and is often found in news reports, eyewitness accounts, or real-time updates about earthquakes, police operations, terrorist threats, evacuations, protests, infrastructure failures, and sudden dramatic turns in events (e.g., “scaraventata sull’asfalto,” “terremoto,” “gás,” “evacua,” “razboi politic”). It also activates on descriptions of intense social or interpersonal tension (e.g., reality TV clashes, romantic confrontations).

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts are largely neutral announcements, routine news summaries, factual reports, or descriptive pieces without a sense of immediacy or crisis (e.g., obituaries, film reviews, healthcare funding, historical discoveries). They lack the urgency, suddenness, or high-stakes drama present in the high-activation examples.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects linguistic markers of real‑time disruption, danger, or heightened emotional stakes, often signaled by action‑oriented verbs, references to emergencies, and context suggesting imminent consequences. It functions as a cross‑lingual “crisis/urgency” sensor in news and narrative text.

results.actual_text: aktör som Expressen visar matcher med många av världens bästa innebandyspelare tror vi att det ger ringar på vat
results.plain_text: Landslagslägret tidigare under året blev inte av. Nu väntar i stället VM-årets första läger i Västerås den 20-23 maj. Det är också första lägret för nya förbundskaptensduon Niklas Nordén och Thomas Brottman. Upplägget blir helt nytt. 33 (30+3) spelare är uttagna, där Kalmarsunds Kevin Haglund är en spännande debutant, och de är uppdelade i Lag Nord och Lag Syd. Lag Nord-Lag Syd i SportExpressen Play I Lag Nord är majoriteten av spelarna från finallagen Falun och Storvreta, som nu alltså kommer finnas i samma lag. I Lag Syd är det en stor variation bland spelarnas klubbtillhörigheter. Bland annat finns André Andersson med. Forwarden är den ende spelaren som till vardags spelar i Schweiz. – Niklas sprutar ur sig häftiga idéer och när han presenterade det här tände jag också på det. Vi vill starta vår gemensamma landslagsresa så att det blir på riktigt direkt. Det blir bättre när det blir matchspel, det blir en extra krydda när det blir något att spela för, säger Thomas Brottman i ett pressmeddelande. Lag Nord och Lag Syd kommer under lägrets gång mötas i tre högintressanta matcher. Dessa fajter kommer att sändas i SportExpressen Play. För att se matcherna behöver man endast skaffa ett kostnadsfritt konto. – Det blir ett utmärkt tillfälle att få flera att upptäcka innebandyn, säger Svensk Innebandys tillförordnande kommunikationschef Anton Rosenblad. ”Det känns hedrande och helt rätt” – När en stor medieaktör som Expressen visar matcher med många av världens bästa innebandyspelare tror vi att det ger ringar på vattnet genom att få flera, både unga och gamla, att intressera sig och engagera sig inom vår sport. Innebandyns världsledande landslag är våra draglok och vi vet att intresset kring dem är stort. Bonnier News livesportansvarige Mattias Wallström: – Vår satsning på innebandy är långsiktig, bland annat genom att vi har sändningsrättigheten för Svenska Superligan i ytterligare fyra år. Det känns hedrande och helt rätt för oss att sända detta och återigen visa upp alla världsstjärnor. Innebandy-VM avgörs på herrsidan i Finland den 30 november med final den 12 december. Sändningstider i SportExpressen Play Lag Nord mot Lag Syd: Match 1: 21/5 kl 18.00 Match 2: 22/5 kl 15.00 Match 3: 23/5 kl 10.00 Truppen till Västerås 20-23 maj Målvakter Jonathan Edling, FC Helsingborg​ Jon Hedlund, Pixbo Wallenstam IBK​ Måns Parsjö-Tegnér IBK, IBK Dalen​ Lag Nord​ Hampus Ahrén, Storvreta IBK​ Omar Aldeeb, IBF Falun​ Christoffer Andersson, IBK Dalen​ (debutant) Rasmus Andersson, IBK Dalen​ Casper Backby , IBF Falun​ Rasmus Enström IBF, Falun​ Alexander Galante Carlström, IBF Falun​ Tobias Gustafsson, Storvreta IBK​ Linus Holmgren, IBK Dalen​ Emil Johansson, IBF Falun​ Malte Lundmark, IBF Falun​ Robin Nilsberth, Storvreta IBK​ Johan Samuelsson, IBF Falun​ Albin Sjögren, Storvreta IBK​ Jonas Svahn, Team Thorengruppen​ Lag Syd​ André Andersson, Zug United​ (Schweiz) Kevin Björkström, FBC Kalmarsund​ Gustav Fritzell, Pixbo Wallenstam IBK Kevin Haglund, FBC Kalmarsund​ (debutant) Oskar Hovlund, Linköping IBK​ Markus Jonsson, Växjö IBK​ Martin Karlsson, Linköping IBK​ Tobias Lindström, FBC Kalmarsund​ Emil Nilsen, Jönköpings IK​ Jonathan Nilsson, FC Helsingborg​ Kim Nilsson, FBC Kalmarsund​ Linus Nordgren, FC Helsingborg​ Ludwig Persson, Växjö IBK​ Niklas Ramirez, Växjö IBK​ Martin Östholm, Pixbo Wallenstam IBK​

results.actual_text: ă la nord-est de Bordholm, iar Nord Stream 2 are o scurgere la sud de zona Dueodde. Seismologul Björ
results.plain_text: Autoritățile ruse consideră "absurde" acuzațiile că şi-ar fi atacat propriile gazoducte care fac legătura cu Germania, mai ales că acestea sunt nefuncţionale.  "Este previzibil ca unii să acuze Rusia. Este previzibil, stupid şi absurd. Fisurile sunt problematice pentru noi, întrucât cele două conducte sunt umplute cu gaz rusesc pregătit să fie pompat, iar acest gaz este scump. Acum acest gaz se scurge", a spus Dmitri Peskov, purtătorul de cuvânt al Kremlinului, scrie Le Monde.  El a declarat, marți, că nu exclude posibilitatea unui act de sabotaj din partea vreunui stat occidental. "Nu poate fi exclusă nicio variantă", a afirmat Peskov. Autorităţile daneze au semnalat fisuri la gazoductele Nord Stream 1 şi Nord Stream 2, care fac legătura între Rusia şi Germania prin Marea Baltică, iar oficialii de la Berlin şi Copenhaga suspectează acte de sabotaj.  Gazoductul Nord Stream 1 are două scurgeri într-o zonă aflată la nord-est de Bordholm, iar Nord Stream 2 are o scurgere la sud de zona Dueodde. Seismologul Björn Lund, citat de postul de televiziune suedez SVT, a declarat că staţiile seismice din Suedia şi Danemarca au înregistrat explozii în zonele în care sunt fisurile.  Guvernul danez şi Josep Borrell Fontelles, Înaltul reprezentant al Uniunii Europene pentru Afaceri Externe şi Politici de Securitate, au afirmat că fisurile sunt rezultatul unui act deliberat. "Daunele la gazoductele Nord Stream 1 şi 2 nu sunt o coincidenţă şi ne afectează pe toţi. Toate informaţiile disponibile indică faptul că fisurile sunt rezultatul unui act deliberat. Perturbarea infrastructurii energetice europene este total inacceptabilă şi va fi urmată de o reacţie solidă şi unitară", a susţinut Fontelles, într-un mesaj postat pe Twiter.  Gazoductul Nord Stream 1 nu este operaţional de trei săptămâni din cauze tehnice invocate de Gazprom, iar conducta Nord Stream 2, construită de Rusia și Germania, nu a fost deschisă din cauza presiunilor exrecitate de SUA asupra Berlinului.

results.actual_text: Ben Cutting could watch Mark Skaife roaring around Mount Panorama for hours. S
results.plain_text: Ben Cutting could watch Mark Skaife roaring around Mount Panorama for hours. Skaife's Holden Commodore whipped around the Esses, the lion badge so often leaving rivals in its wake. But when it came to his own arena, Cutting felt like he lacked that same ferocity. After scoring just 153 runs at an average of 17 and taking one wicket during last summer's BBL, something had to give. So Cutting did some research and found the man who helped Skaife carve out a legacy as one of motorsport's all-time greats: leading sports psychologist Noel Blundell. With a client list that has included the likes of Ash Barty and Minjee Lee, Blundell helped Cutting peel back the layers of his game and return to form for the Sydney Thunder. MORE SPORT: "A lot of it is trying to take yourself back to when you first started playing cricket in the backyard," Cutting said. "You weren't thinking about getting out in the backyard, you were thinking about hitting sixes or hitting runs against your little brother. "He calls it free wheeling, you were just playing because that's what you were doing, you didn't think about anything else. It's about trying to get back to that. "When you have such a long career, scars can creep in, whether that's with the bat, the ball or injuries. It's about trying to dispel those, going back to basics, trusting the process and knowing what you need to do. "The funny thing about cricket is, which Noel is massive on, is not thinking about the outcome because regardless of how you're playing, sometimes it's just not your day. As long as you keep doing those processes, more often than not it will work out at the back end." I worked my arse off seven days a week between PSL and the start of the BBL season. - Thunder all-rounder Ben Cutting Cutting has averaged 30.50 with the bat and picked up two wickets in five games this season, forcing his way into the fold after starting the tournament in the dugout. But the turnaround leading into the Thunder's clash with the Melbourne Renegades at Manuka Oval on Thursday night hasn't just come from conversations with Blundell. Put simply, "I worked my arse off seven days a week between [Pakistan Super League] and the start of the BBL season", linking up with expert bowling coach Joe Dawes. Dawes has been instrumental in one of the biggest changes in Cutting's game this season: slashing his bowling run-up to help the 35-year-old rediscover his touch. "Someone mentioned it to me back in PSL last year, there was probably four or five metres of just cruising to the crease in the run-up. It was a pointless exercise," Cutting said. "I thought about it at the time, but it wasn't until Joey brought it up again. Two sets of fresh eyes saying the same thing means there's probably something in it. I always trust what Joey says. "That's when we took seven metres off the run-up and fiddled with a few other things, and it feels really good. "Between that and the sports psych I've been working with ... It's the best I've felt in a long time." We've made it a whole lot easier for you to have your say. Our new comment platform requires only one log-in to access articles and to join the discussion on The Canberra Times website. Find out how to register so you can enjoy civil, friendly and engaging discussions. See our moderation policy here.

results.actual_text: Sweden’s chief epidemiologist Anders Tegnell has warned against the strategy of attaining
results.plain_text: Sweden’s chief epidemiologist Anders Tegnell has warned against the strategy of attaining herd immunity in apparent change of heart. In March, Dr Tegnell appeared to ask whether a higher death rate among older people might be acceptable if it led to faster herd immunity. However in comments to German newspaper Die Zeit, Dr Tegnell stated “striving for herd immunity is neither ethical nor otherwise justifiable”. Sweden’s daily coronavirus infections have climbed to a new high while several regions advised residents to avoid public transport and venues to slow the transmission. The Swedish Public Health Agency said that 3254 cases were confirmed in the past 24 hours, a new record since the onset of the pandemic. It was the third record number in a matter of days and came as the country of 10.3 million has registered roughly 121,100 infections and 5934 coronavirus-related deaths in total. Sweden has until recently had “a fairly slow increase (of infections) compared to most other countries,” Dr Tegnell said at a press conference. “We’re beginning to approach the ceiling for what the healthcare system can handle. “Together, as during the spring, we can push down this curve and avoid the strain on healthcare.” Dr Tegnell said the Swedish system of “voluntary measures” had worked well and many people heeded the recommendations. The number of COVID-19 patients in intensive care units (ICU) was still relatively low – about 15 per cent of the current ICU capacity, said Thomas Linden with the National Board of Health and Welfare. Sweden has shunned lockdowns throughout the pandemic. However, several regions – including the most populous areas comprising the main cities of Stockholm and Gothenburg in western Sweden – advised residents to refrain from visits to shopping centres, museums, libraries, swimming pools and fitness centres until November 18. Other advice was for people to avoid meeting with others outside their own household and to work from home where possible, Dr Tegnell said. During the pandemic, Sweden has kept schools and kindergartens open but limited public gatherings to 50 participants, visits in care homes were until recently banned and self service in restaurants and bars suspended. -with agencies
